title: narcissus bulbocodium subsp. obesus
intro: Narcissus bulbocodium subsp. obesus, also known as the Hoop Petticoat Daffodil, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Amaryllidaceae family, characterized by its unique, bell-shaped flowers and thick, fleshy bulbs. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine, and its bulbs have been found to contain alkaloids with medicinal properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Narcissus bulbocodium subsp. obesus include its potential to reduce inflammation, act as a diuretic, and exhibit antimicrobial and antispasmodic properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's bulbs are often dried and used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are said to promote digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and soothe nervous conditions.
